title: erythroxylum buxus
intro: Erythroxylum buxus, also known as the box coca, is a plant species native to the Andean region, classified within the Erythroxylum genus. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and lower fever. The health benefits of Erythroxylum buxus include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as arthritis, bronchitis, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent. Erythroxylum buxus can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
